# UC-22.16.4 ¬∑ Unexpected IT-style applications in OT enclaves

## Description

Supports pipeline cybersecurity program oversight under TSA Pipeline Security Directive by correlating operational telemetry with governance records for "Unexpected IT-style applications in OT enclaves".

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for TSA SD compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes ot and sourcetypes pan:traffic. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Confirm field extractions and CIM tags for the sourcetypes in scope; (2) Load or maintain the referenced lookups/KVStore collections with owner attestation dates; (3) Schedule the search at an interval aligned to the control‚Äôs materiality; (4) Route positive findings to the compliance ticketing queue with required evidence fields; (5) Retain scheduled search exports per records management and legal hold procedures. Indexes required: `index=ot`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk OT Security Add-on (5151), Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757), Splunk Industrial Asset Intelligence, Splunk Add-on for CyberArk (4295), Splunk Edge Hub, Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621)..
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: `pan:traffic`, SCADA/HMI audit, PAM, `nozomi:alert`, NetFlow, ITSM, passive OT asset discovery.
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
(1) Confirm field extractions and CIM tags for the sourcetypes in scope; (2) Load or maintain the referenced lookups/KVStore collections with owner attestation dates; (3) Schedule the search at an interval aligned to the control‚Äôs materiality; (4) Route positive findings to the compliance ticketing queue with required evidence fields; (5) Retain scheduled search exports per records management and legal hold procedures.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=ot sourcetype=pan:traffic earliest=-7d zone_dest="OT-PROD"
| eval uc_tag="22.16.4"
| where app IN ("web-browsing","ssl","office365")
| stats count by src_ip, dest_ip, app
| sort - count
```

Understanding this SPL

**Unexpected IT-style applications in OT enclaves (TSA segmentation)** ‚Äî Supports pipeline cybersecurity program oversight under TSA Pipeline Security Directive by correlating operational telemetry with governance records for "Unexpected IT-style applications in OT enclaves".

Documented **Data sources**: `pan:traffic`, SCADA/HMI audit, PAM, `nozomi:alert`, NetFlow, ITSM, passive OT asset discovery. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk OT Security Add-on (5151), Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757), Splunk Industrial Asset Intelligence, Splunk Add-on for CyberArk (4295), Splunk Edge Hub, Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: ot; **sourcetype**: pan:traffic. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Scopes the data: index=ot, sourcetype=pan:traffic, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
‚Ä¢ `eval` defines or adjusts **uc_tag** ‚Äî often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
‚Ä¢ Filters the current rows with `where app IN ("web-browsing","ssl","office365")` ‚Äî typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
‚Ä¢ `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by src_ip, dest_ip, app** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic by All_Traffic.action, All_Traffic.src, All_Traffic.dest, All_Traffic.dest_port | sort - count```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**Unexpected IT-style applications in OT enclaves (TSA segmentation)** ‚Äî Supports pipeline cybersecurity program oversight under TSA Pipeline Security Directive by correlating operational telemetry with governance records for "Unexpected IT-style applications in OT enclaves".

Documented **Data sources**: `pan:traffic`, SCADA/HMI audit, PAM, `nozomi:alert`, NetFlow, ITSM, passive OT asset discovery. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk OT Security Add-on (5151), Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757), Splunk Industrial Asset Intelligence, Splunk Add-on for CyberArk (4295), Splunk Edge Hub, Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Network_Traffic.All_Traffic` ‚Äî enable acceleration for that model.
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Compare with the ESP or perimeter firewall log (for example Palo Alto or FortiGate) for the same window. Spot-check a few source events, reconcile counts, and verify field extractions, thresholds, and index permissions for the people who need this evidence.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Time chart, Choropleth or site bar, Table, Single value

## SPL

```spl
index=ot sourcetype=pan:traffic earliest=-7d zone_dest="OT-PROD"
| eval uc_tag="22.16.4"
| where app IN ("web-browsing","ssl","office365")
| stats count by src_ip, dest_ip, app
| sort - count
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic by All_Traffic.action, All_Traffic.src, All_Traffic.dest, All_Traffic.dest_port | sort - count
```

## Visualization

Time chart, Choropleth or site bar, Table, Single value. Key fields: `app`, `dest_ip`, `src_ip`, `uc_tag`.
